free_text: Measurement of (n,a) reactions on 147Sm and 149Sm
using a lead slowing-down spectrometer
Main reference
Preliminary data
Experiment performed at the RPI
Gaerttner Electron Linear Accelerator Facility 60 MeV
electron linac.
Alpha particles were detected with two
passivated implanted planar silicon (PIPS) detectors.
Detectors were arranged in a light aluminium shell
facing each other. The sample was placed between two
detectors with a 2mm air gap between the sample and
active surface of the detector.
Lead slowing down spectrometer at RPI
consists of lead cube with edge of 1.8 m, weighting
72 metric tons. At the centre of the cube was neutron
producing target, consisting of several air cooled
tantalum plates. Measured sample together with two
detectors was placed in lead channel approx. 20 cm
bellow the target and 40 cm deep.
The average neutron energy is time dependent. Energy
resolution at average neutron energy of 1 keV is
approx. 30%.
Neutrons were produced via (g,n) reaction
by bremsstrahlung photons produced by accelerated
electrons. The neutron source was pulsed.
Pulse shape analysis was performed to eliminate gamma
pile-up and gamma-flash events. The pulse shape
analysis method used in this work imposes two
conditions based on the minimum and maximum pulse
samples. The first condition was the distance between
maximum and minimum of digitized pulse, the second
condition was imposed on the ratio of pulse maximum
to pulse minimum.
Uncertainties were calculated from number
of positive and negative bipolar signal and background
pulses as well as from the uncertainty of the
sample mass.
Compiled by S.H.
US. Minor corrections in subs.2,3
Data presented in fig. 8 of the main reference
sent by author (J.T.T.)
Sample 147Sm in chemical form oxide with mass of
(9.6+-0.05)mg, area of (1.45+-0.08)cm2 and thickness

equation
Sigma = A x E**m
using the nonlinear, iterative Levenberg-Marquardt
method and extrapolated to thermal energy
